title: [arcilator] Failed to legalize 'sim.clocked_terminate' and dangling macro references

features.name: sv.macro.decl with sv.macro.ref.expr outside always block
features.description: Code should include a SystemVerilog macro declaration (`sv.macro.decl`) referenced by a macro expression (`sv.macro.ref.expr`) that is consumed outside of an `sv.always` block, such as by a `sim.clocked_terminate` operation.
features.snippet:
sv.macro.decl @STOP_COND_
  %STOP_COND_ = sv.macro.ref.expr @STOP_COND_() : () -> i1
  %1 = comb.and bin %STOP_COND_, %cond : i1
  sim.clocked_terminate %clock, %1, success, verbose

features.name: dangling macro reference after StripSV pass
features.description: Code should include a macro reference expression that remains after the StripSV pass removes the corresponding `sv.macro.decl`, resulting in a dangling reference and undefined symbol error.

features.name: sim.clocked_terminate with condition operand
features.description: Code should include a `sim.clocked_terminate` operation in MLIR that takes a clock value and a boolean condition operand, representing a missing lowering target in arcilator.

features.name: SystemVerilog stop() statement
features.description: Code should include a SystemVerilog `stop()` statement that triggers simulation termination when executed, which may lower to a `sim.clocked_terminate` operation in hardware compilers.

features.name: SystemVerilog assert() with clocked termination
features.description: Code should include a SystemVerilog `assert()` statement evaluated on a clock edge that can lower to a clocked simulation termination construct in a hardware compiler.
